sourceProvenance.adaptationNotice: JStack adapted the source checklist into applicability-aware engineering controls. Source priorities are retained as provenance, while JStack gate levels, evidence rules, safety boundaries, and conditional applicability are independent engineering judgments.

surfaces.description: Every production software change, including services without a public browser surface.
An internet-reachable website, application, API, or documentation surface.
A user interface rendered and operated in a web browser.
A surface with login, authorization, entitlements, or paywall decisions.
A change that stores or reads application data from a database.
A product that sends signup, account, billing, or other transactional email.
A public surface intended to be discovered through search engines.
A user-facing surface with an explicit latency or web-performance expectation.
A product that emits analytics events or product measurement data.
A product that initiates, receives, or reconciles monetary transactions.
A product offered to customers, subscribers, or paying users.
A product using cookies, recordings, advertising, or non-essential behavioral tracking.
An endpoint whose abuse creates meaningful AI, vendor, or compute cost.
A product handling personal, financial, health, or otherwise regulated data.

controls.title: Enforce database row-level access boundaries
controls.objective: Prove that anonymous and cross-tenant access cannot read or mutate protected database rows outside the intended policy.
verificationMethods: Inspect effective database policies
Run negative anonymous and cross-tenant access tests
Record any privileged-service bypass boundary
controls.safetyNotes: A framework setting or migration file alone is not proof that the effective production policy denies unintended access.

controls.title: Enforce authentication and entitlements on the server
controls.objective: Prove that protected data and actions remain inaccessible when browser checks, routes, or client state are bypassed.
verificationMethods: Trace authorization at the authoritative server boundary
Run unauthenticated and wrong-role negative tests
Verify paywall or entitlement decisions cannot be client-forged
controls.safetyNotes: Client-side route guards and hidden controls are usability features, not authorization evidence.

controls.title: Verify environment and diagnostic routes expose no secrets
controls.objective: Confirm that common environment, debug, source-map, backup, and diagnostic paths reveal no configuration or credentials.
verificationMethods: Probe common environment and backup paths
Review production error and debug behavior
Confirm source maps and diagnostics contain no secrets
controls.safetyNotes: Evidence must record status and hashes, not secret values or raw sensitive responses.

controls.title: Keep privileged secrets out of client-delivered assets
controls.objective: Prove that browser bundles, source maps, static assets, logs, and public configuration contain no privileged credential.
verificationMethods: Scan built client artifacts
Trace privileged configuration to server-only boundaries
Verify exposed credentials are rotated rather than merely removed
controls.safetyNotes: Public client identifiers may be acceptable only when their privilege model is documented and server-side controls remain authoritative.

controls.title: Enforce HTTPS and validate the deployed certificate
controls.objective: Verify secure transport, redirect behavior, certificate validity, and the absence of mixed active content on the intended public origin.
verificationMethods: Validate certificate chain and hostname
Verify HTTP redirects safely to HTTPS
Check browser-visible mixed-content failures
controls.safetyNotes: Do not weaken certificate verification in order to make a launch check pass.

controls.title: Rate-limit endpoints that create material cost or abuse risk
controls.objective: Prove that expensive AI, vendor, compute, or mutation endpoints have identity-aware limits and bounded failure behavior.
verificationMethods: Exercise burst and sustained-rate limits
Verify limits cannot be bypassed by trivial identity changes
Confirm rejection paths do not invoke the costly dependency
controls.safetyNotes: A client-side debounce or button disable is not a server-side abuse boundary.

controls.title: Configure and verify SPF, DKIM, and DMARC
controls.objective: Confirm that the deployed sending domain has aligned authentication records and an intentional DMARC policy.
verificationMethods: Inspect live DNS records
Verify message authentication headers
Confirm alignment between visible From and authenticated domains
controls.safetyNotes: Record results without storing mailbox credentials, provider keys, or full message bodies.

controls.title: Exercise every launch-critical transactional email
controls.objective: Verify that required account and commercial email paths are triggered, delivered, rendered, linked, and recoverable.
verificationMethods: Trigger each required template through its real event path
Verify links and expiry behavior
Confirm bounce or provider failure is observable
controls.safetyNotes: Use designated test accounts and redact personal content from retained evidence.

controls.title: Verify delivery and rendering in independent mailbox providers
controls.objective: Confirm at least Gmail and Outlook-class clients receive and render a representative transactional message correctly.
verificationMethods: Deliver to two independent mailbox providers
Inspect spam placement and rendering
Exercise primary calls to action in both clients
controls.safetyNotes: A provider API acceptance response is not evidence of inbox delivery.

controls.title: Isolate transactional sending reputation when appropriate
controls.objective: Document and verify the chosen sender-domain boundary so application email does not unintentionally share unrelated reputation or DNS ownership.
verificationMethods: Record the sending-domain decision
Verify provider and DNS configuration match it
Confirm reply and return-path behavior
controls.safetyNotes: A subdomain is a conditional architecture choice, not a universal requirement; not-applicable evidence must explain the equivalent isolation boundary.

controls.title: Run an independent deliverability quality check
controls.objective: Use a reputable diagnostic service or equivalent evidence to identify authentication, content, and reputation problems before launch.
verificationMethods: Run a bounded third-party or equivalent deliverability diagnostic
Review warnings rather than relying on a score alone
controls.safetyNotes: No single vendor score is a release guarantee; provider-specific thresholds remain advisory.

controls.title: Provide a valid social link preview
controls.objective: Verify that the canonical public entry point exposes a reachable preview image and coherent Open Graph metadata.
verificationMethods: Fetch metadata as an unauthenticated crawler
Validate absolute image URL and dimensions
Inspect a representative rendered preview
controls.safetyNotes: The preview asset must not disclose staging URLs, private content, or user data.

controls.title: Publish and submit the intended sitemap
controls.objective: Verify that the sitemap is valid, canonical, limited to public URLs, and submitted to the intended search property.
verificationMethods: Fetch and parse the live sitemap
Check canonical production origins
Record search-console submission or equivalent
controls.safetyNotes: Exclude authenticated, staging, administrative, and user-specific URLs.

controls.title: Confirm intended pages are not blocked from indexing
controls.objective: Verify production robots, headers, metadata, authentication, and canonical tags do not contradict the declared indexing intent.
verificationMethods: Inspect robots directives and X-Robots-Tag headers
Check canonical and noindex metadata
Fetch representative pages without a privileged session
controls.safetyNotes: Do not make private or authenticated pages indexable merely to satisfy this control.

controls.title: Give every public page purposeful title and description metadata
controls.objective: Verify public routes have non-placeholder, route-appropriate titles, descriptions, and canonical identity.
verificationMethods: Crawl public routes for missing or duplicate metadata
Inspect dynamic route fallbacks
Check canonical URLs
controls.safetyNotes: Generated metadata must not expose private route parameters or personal data.

controls.title: Remove localhost, staging, and placeholder references
controls.objective: Confirm production-visible code, metadata, manifests, links, and runtime configuration contain no unintended development or staging identity.
verificationMethods: Scan built and served assets for development origins
Exercise redirects and callback URLs
Inspect public manifests and metadata
controls.safetyNotes: Allowlisted development references must be non-production code paths with documented guards.

controls.title: Evaluate application and marketing domain separation
controls.objective: Record whether separating application and marketing surfaces improves security, routing, ownership, or search behavior for this product.
verificationMethods: Document the domain topology decision
Review cookie, authentication, and canonical-URL effects
controls.safetyNotes: Domain separation is conditional and must not be presented as a universal quality requirement.

controls.title: Publish an intentional crawler policy
controls.objective: Verify robots.txt accurately expresses public crawler policy; document separately whether an optional llms.txt file adds value.
verificationMethods: Fetch and parse robots.txt
Check sitemap references and protected paths
Record the optional llms.txt decision without treating it as a standard
controls.safetyNotes: llms.txt is advisory and must not replace access control, robots directives, or documented API policy.

controls.title: Capture a reproducible page-performance baseline
controls.objective: Measure the intended production-like pages with a declared tool, environment, sample, and actionable performance budget.
verificationMethods: Run PageSpeed Insights, Lighthouse, or a documented equivalent
Record representative routes and device profile
Compare results with an explicit budget
controls.safetyNotes: A single score is not enough; retain workload, environment, and metric evidence.

controls.title: Optimize production image payloads
controls.objective: Verify that user-facing images are appropriately sized, compressed, encoded, and loaded for their rendered use.
verificationMethods: Inspect oversized source-to-rendered dimensions
Check compression and modern format use
Verify lazy loading does not break critical content
controls.safetyNotes: Tool choice is not prescribed; Squoosh is one option rather than a gate dependency.

controls.title: Prevent disruptive layout movement
controls.objective: Measure and review layout shifts on critical routes so late content does not move controls or primary content unexpectedly.
verificationMethods: Capture cumulative layout shift
Exercise delayed images, fonts, banners, and dynamic content
Review critical interaction movement visually
controls.safetyNotes: Do not hide content or disable accessibility features merely to improve a metric.

controls.title: Remove unused generated or inherited dependencies
controls.objective: Review declared and bundled dependencies for unused packages, accidental AI scaffolding, duplicate functionality, and avoidable supply-chain exposure.
verificationMethods: Compare declared dependencies with imports and runtime use
Inspect bundle or package output
Re-run tests after bounded removals
controls.safetyNotes: Do not remove dynamically loaded or build-time dependencies solely because a static tool reports them unused.

controls.title: Verify analytics events are delivered with the intended consent state
controls.objective: Confirm the production-like client or service emits the agreed events once, with valid names, properties, identity handling, and consent behavior.
verificationMethods: Inspect provider debug or live-event views
Exercise consent accepted and denied states
Check duplicate and missing events on navigation
controls.safetyNotes: Retained evidence must not contain raw personal identifiers, session tokens, or full event payloads.

controls.title: Collect actionable web-vitals telemetry
controls.objective: Verify that agreed browser-performance metrics reach an observable destination with route and release context sufficient for diagnosis.
verificationMethods: Generate representative browser vitals
Confirm metrics reach the intended property
Verify release and route context without personal data
controls.safetyNotes: Telemetry cardinality and personal-data collection must remain bounded.

controls.title: Protect public and authenticated entry points from basic automation abuse
controls.objective: Verify proportionate bot controls on signup, login, forms, and costly public actions without creating inaccessible or unreviewed lockouts.
verificationMethods: Exercise repeated and scripted requests
Verify server-side enforcement and observable rejections
Review accessibility and false-positive recovery
controls.safetyNotes: CAPTCHA is not mandatory; the control requires a proportionate, testable abuse boundary.

controls.title: Define and verify one decision-useful conversion funnel
controls.objective: Confirm that a primary public or commercial journey has stable step definitions, emitted events, and a queryable funnel view.
verificationMethods: Document funnel step definitions
Exercise a complete journey
Confirm the completed journey appears without duplicate steps
controls.safetyNotes: A vanity dashboard is not evidence unless the funnel supports an identified product decision.

controls.title: Verify production error tracking and release correlation
controls.objective: Prove that representative handled and unhandled failures reach an owned alerting or triage surface with release context and safe redaction.
verificationMethods: Generate a designated test failure
Confirm capture and release correlation
Inspect redaction and ownership
Verify an actionable alert or review path
controls.safetyNotes: Do not generate uncontrolled production failures; use a safe test path or staging-equivalent evidence when production mutation is not separately authorized.

controls.title: Gate session recording behind consent and redaction
controls.objective: If session recording is used, verify consent, masking, exclusion rules, retention, and user opt-out behavior before collection.
verificationMethods: Exercise consent denied and accepted paths
Inspect masking of inputs and private pages
Verify retention and deletion settings
controls.safetyNotes: The control does not recommend session recording; it constrains recording when the product chooses to use it.

controls.title: Publish owned terms and privacy disclosures
controls.objective: Confirm applicable terms and privacy documents exist, match the actual product and data practices, are reachable, and have an accountable owner.
verificationMethods: Record the accountable legal or business owner
Verify published links and version dates
Reconcile disclosed processors and data flows with the implementation
controls.safetyNotes: JStack can coordinate evidence but cannot provide legal advice or certify jurisdictional sufficiency.

controls.title: Identify the merchant of record and operational obligations
controls.objective: Record who legally sells to the customer and who owns tax, invoicing, refunds, disputes, and customer-support obligations.
verificationMethods: Name the merchant of record
Reconcile checkout and receipt identity
Document refund, dispute, tax, and invoice ownership
controls.safetyNotes: This is an accountable business determination; JStack must not infer or decide it from provider branding alone.

controls.title: Implement the applicable tracking-consent decision
controls.objective: Verify the product's documented jurisdictional decision is reflected in cookie, storage, tag, withdrawal, and consent-record behavior.
verificationMethods: Record the accountable legal decision
Exercise accept, reject, and withdraw paths
Inspect storage and network activity before consent
controls.safetyNotes: JStack validates implementation against an owned decision; it does not decide which jurisdictions or legal bases apply.

controls.title: Verify live payment webhook behavior safely
controls.objective: Prove the intended live-mode payment event is authenticated, idempotent, reconciled, retried, and observable without duplicate fulfillment.
verificationMethods: Verify signature validation and replay handling
Exercise one approved live or provider-supported equivalent event
Confirm idempotent fulfillment and reconciliation
Inspect retry and failure visibility
controls.safetyNotes: JStack launch tools never authorize real-money actions or production mutation; any live exercise requires separate explicit authority, a bounded amount, rollback, and reconciliation plan.

controls.title: Test an independent browser and desktop/mobile viewport
controls.objective: Verify critical flows in at least two independent browser engines or environments and representative desktop and mobile layouts.
verificationMethods: Run critical paths in two browser environments
Exercise desktop and mobile breakpoints
Record unsupported combinations explicitly
controls.safetyNotes: A responsive emulator is useful evidence but does not fully replace a real device for device-specific risk.

controls.title: Walk the core user flow on a phone-class device
controls.objective: Complete the primary user journey with touch input, mobile viewport constraints, network variability, and mobile keyboard behavior.
verificationMethods: Complete the primary journey on a phone-class environment
Check touch targets, keyboard, overlays, and orientation
Observe failure and retry behavior on a constrained network
controls.safetyNotes: Use designated test identities and avoid retaining screenshots containing personal or payment data.

controls.title: Exercise every intended link and interactive control
controls.objective: Crawl or manually inspect the bounded launch surface for dead links, inert controls, wrong destinations, duplicate submission, and inaccessible actions.
verificationMethods: Crawl links and buttons within the declared surface
Exercise disabled, loading, and duplicate-click states
Verify keyboard and touch activation
controls.safetyNotes: Automation must exclude destructive actions unless a safe test environment and explicit authority exist.

controls.title: Challenge forms with invalid, boundary, and hostile input
controls.objective: Verify validation, encoding, error recovery, duplicate submission, authorization, and safe logging across user-input boundaries.
verificationMethods: Test empty, oversized, malformed, Unicode, and duplicate input
Exercise server-side validation and authorization
Check error messages and logs for sensitive echo
controls.safetyNotes: Run destructive payloads only in an isolated authorized environment; never target unrelated systems.

controls.title: Verify safe and useful not-found behavior
controls.objective: Confirm unknown routes return the intended status, expose no internal detail, preserve observability, and help users recover.
verificationMethods: Request unknown public and asset routes
Check status, content, navigation, and metadata
Verify internal paths and stack traces remain hidden
controls.safetyNotes: A branded error page must still return an appropriate HTTP status and avoid leaking internal routing detail.
